raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Youth poverty in California increased from 9.0% in fall 2021 to 13.8% in early 2023.",
    "The Latino poverty rate in California rose to 16.9% from 13.5% in fall 2021.",
    "Latinos make up about 50.7% of poor Californians while comprising only 39.7% of the total population.",
    "Social safety net programs have been effective in preventing poverty for 14.9% of children in early 2023.",
    "Prolonged exposure to poverty can significantly shape children's development, leading to heightened stress and poor school performance.",
    "The status quo regarding childhood poverty in California is deemed unacceptable.",
    "Youth poverty is described as a policy choice made by the legislature and the Governor.",
    "The proposed ACA 21 bill seeks to authorize taxing candy to raise revenue aimed at ending youth poverty.",
    "The proposed AB 2977 bill aims to revise current tax credits to strengthen the California Youth Childhood Tax Credit (YCTC)."
  ]
}
```
